metadata.notes: Includes an option to return to an earlier step in the normal flow.

inputs.vague: A logged-in user, either a Spirit Director or a SuperFrog Student, wants to look up appearance requests that meet certain conditions. The user enters one or more search values and the system returns a filtered list of matching requests, displayed and sorted according to defined strategies. If no results are found, the user can start a new request or refine the search. The user can also customize which fields are shown or re-sort the results.
inputs.detailed:
Primary Actor:
User (Spirit Director or SuperFrog Student)
Secondary Actors:


Trigger:
The User indicates to find appearance requests.
Description:
The User wants to find appearance requests which match specific criteria, so that she can decide what to do next.
Preconditions:
PRE-1. The User is logged into the System.
Postconditions:
POST-1. A list of matching appearance requests is returned and displayed to the User. It is possible that the list is empty.
Main Success Scenario:
The User indicates to find appearance requests.
The System asks the User to enter search values according to the “Search criteria” defined in the Associated Information of this use case.
The User enters one or more search values and confirms that she has finished entering.
The System finds all appearance requests that match the provided search criteria values.
The System displays the matching appearance requests according to the “Search results display strategy” and the “Sort criteria” defined in the Associated Information of this use case.
Use case ends.
Extensions:
4a. No matching requests are found:
4a1. The System alerts the User that no matching appearance requests are found.
4a2. The User either chooses to UC-5: Request a SuperFrog appearance for TCU events or chooses to terminate the use case or chooses to return to step 2 of the normal flow.
5a. The User chooses to select a different set of properties to display the matching appearance requests:
5a1. The System displays the current “Search results display strategy”.
5a2. The User enters a customized “Search results display strategy”, confirms that she has finished entering, and returns to step 5 of the normal flow.
5b. The User chooses to re-sort the search results:
5b1. The User re-sorts the search result according to the “Sort criteria” defined in the Associated Information of this use case and returns to step 5 of the normal flow.

groundTruth.name: Find Appearance Requests
groundTruth.summary: The User wants to find appearance requests which match specific criteria, so that she can decide what to do next.
preconditions: The User is logged into the System.

steps.description: Asks the User to enter search values according to the “Search criteria”.
Enters one or more search values and confirms that she has finished entering.
Finds all appearance requests that match the provided search criteria values.
Displays the matching appearance requests according to the “Search results display strategy” and the “Sort criteria”.
Alerts the User that no matching appearance requests are found.
Either chooses to UC-5: Request a SuperFrog appearance for TCU events or chooses to terminate the use case or chooses to return to step 2 of the normal flow.
Displays the current “Search results display strategy”.
Enters a customized “Search results display strategy”, confirms that she has finished entering, and returns to step 5 of the normal flow.
Re-sorts the search result according to the “Sort criteria” and returns to step 5 of the normal flow.
